description_long: This is a traditional broom with a long, slender wooden handle and a bundle of natural bristles tightly bound at one end. The handle appears to be made of a single piece of wood, showing a slightly weathered, matte finish with a cylindrical cross-section. The bristle end is conical, with the bristles fanning out slightly and secured by a visible binding, likely twine or wire, wrapped around the base. The bristles are uneven in length, giving the sweeping end a rough, organic appearance. The color palette consists of muted browns and yellows, typical of natural wood and straw materials. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, and the broom shows some signs of use, particularly at the bristle tips.
description: A classic wooden-handled broom with natural, fanned bristles bound at one end, showing a rustic, well-used appearance.
description_view_0: The broom is shown at an angle, highlighting the full length of the handle and the conical, fanned bristle end.
description_view_1: A direct view of the bristle end, showing the circular arrangement and density of the bristles.
description_view_2: The broom is displayed from the side, emphasizing the straight handle and the spread of the bristles.
description_view_3: A view from the handle end, focusing on the cylindrical shape and the transition to the bristle bundle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a round cross-section and no visible grip enhancements or end caps.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: Bristles are densely packed, fanned out in a conical shape, with visible variation in length and a rough, uneven tip; bound tightly with twine at the base.
attribute_mounting_position: Designed to be leaned against a surface or hung vertically by the handle; no dedicated mounting feature visible.
attribute_wear_pattern: Visible wear at the bristle tips, including fraying and slight discoloration; handle shows minor surface wear but no significant damage.
